name: Al Klink
abstract: Al Klink (December 28, 1915 in Danbury, Connecticut – March 7, 1991 in Bradenton, Florida) was an American swing jazz tenor saxophonist.

works.t: Strike Up the Band: The Man I Love
Swingin’ the Dream: Darn that Dream

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: And I’m sitting there with Al Klink who played with Glenn Miller, and Clark Terry, Maxwell, Jimmy Maxwell, Snooky Young. I mean that band was incredible.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: Al Klink is mentioned by the interviewer as a musician on a record, but the interviewee (Cozy Cole) does not respond with any personal connection or confirmation of working with him.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder
about_quotes.evidence: Al Klink. He started with them and began to get a tremendous audience... That’s what he did. I played a number of those things.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Haggart
about_quotes.evidence: I know one of the guys in the band was Al Klink and he hated that “In the Mood” so much that you wouldn’t believe it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Haggart interviewed by Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: John Best
about_quotes.evidence: Tex was in the band. Al Klink, Willie Schwartz, Hal McIntyre.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Best interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998
